title: America s AI chip rules keep changing and the rest of the world is paying the price
summary: The US is changing its export control rules for AI chips, impacting global trade and technology sharing. Experts like Chris McGuire are discussing the implications of these shifting policies. The Trump Administration's stance on AI accelerator exports is in flux.
insight: US AI chip rules uncertainty hampers global technological collaboration and competition.

title: Artemis II astronaut finds two Outlook instances running on computers, calls on Houston to fix Microsoft anomaly puzzled caller describes two Outlooks, and neither one of those are working
summary: Artemis II astronaut reports two non-functional Microsoft Outlook instances on space station computers.
insight: Software malfunction can compromise critical space missions and operations.

title: Denuvo has been broken, company promises countermeasures against new DRM bypasses zero-day game releases become norm as security concerns mount over hypervisor-based bypass
summary: Denuvo, a popular DRM solution, has been broken by recent hypervisor-based bypasses, prompting the company to promise countermeasures. The company aims to prevent future zero-day exploits, which have become a norm in the gaming industry. The industry is concerned about the security implications of these bypasses.
insight: Gaming industry security is compromised by hypervisor-based DRM bypasses.

title: China's homegrown silicon suppliers gain traction as Nvidia struggles to get its chips into the market Huawei, Cambricon and more step up to fill crucial market gap
summary: Nvidia's market share in China's data center market is declining, with homegrown suppliers like Huawei and Cambricon gaining traction and increasing their combined market share to 41%. This shift is a result of the ongoing US-China tech tensions and Nvidia's struggles to supply its chips to the Chinese market. The growth of Chinese suppliers is expected to continue.
insight: Rise of Chinese AI chipmakers challenges Nvidia's global dominance.

title: IBM spruces up its mainframes with new support for modern Arm workloads firm teams up with Arm to run Arm workloads on IBM Z mainframes
summary: IBM is enhancing its mainframes to support modern Arm workloads, partnering with Arm to run these workloads on IBM Z and LinuxONE systems. This move aims to make IBM's machines more relevant for today's AI and data-intensive workloads. Cloud-native applications will now be compatible with IBM's mainframes.
insight: Mainframe relevance restored with Arm-based cloud-native workload support

title: Artemis II will use laser beams to live-stream 4K moon footage at 260 Mbps one giant step beyond the S-band radio comms of the Apollo era
summary: NASA's Artemis II will live-stream 4K moon footage via laser beams at 260 Mbps, surpassing the resolution and speed of the Apollo era's S-band radio comms.
insight: Revolutionizing space communication with high-speed, low-latency laser transmission technology.

title: Ubuntu Linux raises minimum system memory requirements by 50% requirements bumped to 6GB of RAM, previously raised from 1GB to 4GB in 2018
summary: Ubuntu 26.04 LTS raises minimum system memory requirements by 50% to 6GB RAM.
insight: Impact on low-RAM devices and users with outdated hardware configurations

title: 1Hz laptop display reportedly helps deliver outstanding 43-hour battery life on the XPS 14 during web browser use beats the M5 MacBook Air by more than 28 hours in the same test
summary: The Dell XPS 14 boasts exceptional battery life thanks to its 1Hz display, outperforming the M5 MacBook Air by nearly 30 hours in web browser tests, delivering up to 43 hours of battery life.
insight: 1Hz display technology significantly boosts laptop battery life

title: GameStop NVMe SSD 2TB SSD Review: A surprisingly capable budget drive for the PS5
summary: The GameStop NVMe SSD is a decent gamer-oriented drive with good performance and a nice heatsink at a reasonable price. SMI and Samsung hardware come together for a compelling package.

title: Amazing $490 Newegg combo includes an AMD Ryzen 5 9600X CPU, 16GB of G.Skill DDR5-6000 RAM, MSI Pro B850-S motherboard, and 240mm MSI MAG Coreliquid A13 AIO CPU cooler all for under $500
summary: Save under $500 on an AMD Ryzen 5 9600X bundle from Newegg.
insight: Budget PC builders can now access high-performance components cheaply.

title: Modders use jumper wires and a custom BIOS to save a damaged RTX 4090 from the trash resurrected Nvidia gaming GPU loses 4GB of VRAM to overcome terminal PCB sagging
summary: A YouTuber used a custom BIOS to revive a damaged RTX 4090 with a bent PCB, reducing its VRAM from 24GB to 20GB. This process stabilized the GPU and allowed it to function. The modder utilized jumper wires to bypass the damaged area.
insight: Creative repurposing of damaged hardware can extend its lifespan and usefulness.

title: Why Nvidia just poured $2 billion into AI ASIC competitor Marvell NVLink Fusion turns into soft ecosystem lock-in
summary: Nvidia has invested $2 billion in Marvell and formed a partnership through NVLink Fusion, potentially expanding their reach in the AI ASIC market.
insight: Nvidia secures AI ASIC dominance through strategic ecosystem partnership.

title: Global semiconductor foundry market hit a record $320 billion in 2025 as TSMC pulled further ahead
summary: The global semiconductor foundry market hit a record $320 billion in revenue in 2025, with a 16% year-over-year growth, driven by increasing demand for semiconductors in various industries.
insight: Semiconductor demand drives record-breaking foundry market growth momentum

title: Raspberry Pi flagship 500+ model now costs almost as much as a Mac Mini firm Pi launches 3GB model to fight increasing DRAM prices
summary: Raspberry Pi prices have risen due to the ongoing DRAM shortage. The flagship 500+ 16GB model has seen a nearly 50% price increase. To combat this, a new 3GB model has been launched.
insight: Rising DRAM prices impact affordability of single-board computers.

title: SupTronics X1208 A UPS + M.2 NVMe SSD HAT for the Raspberry Pi 5
summary: The SupTronics X1208 is a UPS and M.2 NVMe SSD HAT designed for the Raspberry Pi 5, providing fast storage and power protection during outages.
insight: Raspberry Pi 5 systems now have built-in power protection and storage

title: Sentinel Core A Raspberry Pi CM5 mini-ITX carrier board with a PCIe x16 slot (Crowdfunding)
summary: The Sentinel Core is a Raspberry Pi CM5 carrier board with a PCIe x16 slot, allowing users to connect a graphics card for accelerated 3D graphics, video transcoding, or AI workloads. It also features HDMI ports, a Gigabit Ethernet port, USB ports, and a prototyping area. This carrier board supports the Raspberry Pi Compute Module 5.
insight: Accelerates AI, 3D graphics, and video workloads with PCIe x16 slot

title: Nuvoton NuMicro M3331 Cortex-M33 MCU features built-in ARGB LED controller, optional USB 2.0 OTG interface
summary: Nuvoton's NuMicro M3331 MCU features a built-in ARGB LED controller and optional USB 2.0 OTG interface. It's a 32-bit Arm Cortex-M33 MCU clocked at 180 MHz with TrustZone security and a single-precision FPU. This MCU is suitable for smart factories, renewable energy systems, and consumer devices.
insight: Offloads CPU for fluid LED effects in gaming applications

title: Mekotronics R57-5S Rockchip RK3576 mini PC and digital player integrates inclined 5-inch touchscreen display
summary: The Mekotronics R57-5S is a Rockchip RK3576 mini PC with a built-in 5-inch touchscreen display, designed for kiosks and digital signage applications. It offers various storage and connectivity options, including HDMI 2.1 and USB-C DP outputs. The system also features an AI accelerator and terminal block interfaces.
insight: Enhanced digital signage with AI capabilities and touchscreen interactivity

title: Humanity returns to the Moon, but Outlook still doesn't work
summary: NASA's historic Moon return mission encountered an unexpected issue: a malfunctioning software, Outlook, which added a relatable moment to the historic launch.
insight: Humanity's technological advancements still struggle with everyday software issues.

title: Dell's new XPS 14 dominates MacBook Air with 43 hours of battery life in web browsing test
summary: Dell's XPS 14 laptop boasts exceptional battery life, lasting 43 hours on a single charge while browsing the web with variable refresh rate enabled.
insight: Long battery life redefines mobile productivity expectations for professionals and travelers

title: Developer rebuilds Doom using only CSS and HTML, turning divs into a full 3D game engine
summary: Developer Leenheer recreated Doom using CSS and HTML, turning divs into a 3D game engine. He leveraged code from an earlier experiment that extracted map data from the original WAD file. This project showcases the potential of CSS and HTML for complex game development.
insight: CSS and HTML can power complex 3D game engines.

title: Hitachi and MOL plan to turn retired ships into floating data centers
summary: Mitsui O.S.K. Lines and Hitachi plan to repurpose retired ships into data centers, aiming to improve convenience and efficiency in the data center business.
insight: Floating data centers reduce land-based data center environmental impact.

title: This chip keeps working at 700 C, surviving lava-like heat
summary: USC researchers developed a memory device that withstands extreme heat up to 700°C, far surpassing previous thermal limits.
insight: Advancements in heat-resistant electronics could revolutionize high-temperature applications.

title: Ubisoft pulled The Crew offline, and now it's facing a lawsuit
summary: Ubisoft pulled The Crew's servers offline without warning, sparking a lawsuit. French consumer group UFC-Que Choisir claims Ubisoft's decision stripped consumers of their rights. The group filed the lawsuit due to Ubisoft's lack of transparency about the game's expiration date.
insight: Lack of transparency in games' expiration dates causes consumer frustration

title: NASA launches Artemis II, its first crewed mission toward the Moon in decades
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission launched four astronauts on a nine-day loop around the Moon.
insight: Historic step toward returning humans to the lunar surface by 2025.

title: USPTO rejects Nintendo's "summon and fight" Pok mon patent as Palworld battle continues
summary: USPTO rejects Nintendo's 'summon and fight' Pokémon patent in a significant blow to their IP.
insight: Nintendo's patent claims may be too broad to be granted protection.

title: SpaceX quietly files for massive IPO that could value the company at over $1.75 trillion
summary: SpaceX reportedly files for a massive IPO that could value the company at over $1.75 trillion, potentially surpassing Saudi Aramco's 2019 IPO record.
insight: Potential valuation could redefine the private space industry's financial landscape forever.

title: Memory spiked first, CPUs followed, now PCBs could be the next victim of the AI boom
summary: TrendForce reports DRAM and NAND costs will rise in Q2 2026 due to AI data center demand. Shortages of basic materials may increase printed circuit board prices. This could impact electronics manufacturing.
insight: AI-driven demand disrupts traditional electronics supply chain dynamics.

title: Hoax post claiming the world's oldest tortoise had died was a crypto scam
summary: A hoax post claiming the world's oldest tortoise had died was a crypto scam. The post, attributed to a vet, fooled several large media outlets. It was used to promote a cryptocurrency.
insight: Social media manipulation can be used for financial scams effectively

title: Quantum computers might crack today's encryption far sooner than we thought
summary: A study by Caltech and UC engineers found that quantum computers can break advanced encryption using relatively less powerful machines than previously thought, threatening the security of RSA public-key encryption.
insight: Vulnerability to quantum computers undermines global encryption security standards.

title: March Steam survey: Intel Arc finally breaks into the charts as AMD and Linux reach record shares
summary: Intel Arc GPU finally breaks into the Steam survey charts. AMD and Linux reach record shares in the latest Steam survey. February's results show significant changes from the previous month.
insight: Emerging competition in the GPU market drives significant market shifts

title: Apple turns 50 with a legacy of reinventing the personal computer
summary: Apple has reinvented the personal computer over five decades, transforming its design and capabilities.
insight: Continuous innovation drives Apple's enduring success in the tech industry.
